response: ["Take a few minutes to practice the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique: name 5 things you can see, 4 you can touch, 3 you can hear, 2 you can smell, and 1 you can taste — this anchors you to the present moment when shock makes your mind race.","Allow yourself to sit with the initial shock without making any major decisions right away, giving your nervous system time to regulate before you react, since choices made in the heat of surprise are often ones we later regret.","Reach out to one trusted person and simply tell them what happened — you don't need advice yet, just say 'I just got hit with something I didn't see coming and I need someone to talk to,' as verbalizing the experience helps your brain begin to process it.","Try slow box breathing to calm your body's stress response: inhale for 4 counts, hold for 4, exhale for 4, and hold for 4 — repeat this 4–5 times to reduce the flood of adrenaline that comes with being blindsided.","Once the initial shock settles, write down everything you know for certain versus what you're assuming, as separating facts from fears can help you regain a sense of clarity and control in a situation that feels completely disorienting."]
